hero.title: TASA.WORKS
hero.note:
Welcome the room
Hold one breath on the image

intro.title: Thomas Anton Sekula
intro.note:
Short introduction
Started my own freelance practice — architectural design and rendering
Ten years studying classical architecture at the University of Notre Dame
Following my studies, worked under Craig Hamilton
Later worked for Mark Appleton in my hometown of Santa Barbara
Now working freelance, directly pursuing my design passions

history.title: background
history.note: A quick overview of my traditional design history and work

inkwash.note: I appreciate the discipline of traditional ink wash rendering

watercolor.note: Plein air watercolor paintingSpread — two vertical watercolors

model3d1.note: My work also heavily involves digital 3D modeling

sketchbook.note: More broadly, I love drawing — constantly keeping a sketchbook

pivot.title: Computational Media
pivot.note:
During COVID I discovered modular synthesizers and electronic music production
Spent hours patching complex, generative systems on the hardware
That logic translated perfectly into node-based AI image generation

synth.note:
Left — a complex physical modular synthesizer patch
Right — a ComfyUI node network, side by side

ai1.note:
Two years diving straight into advanced AI image creation
This year — moved away from programs, started writing my own that just do what I want

ai2.note: What deeply resonates with people is its ability to mimic traditional media

medium.title: IMAGE AND MEANING
medium.note:
I used to speak with Leo Casas at length about traditional construction
The type of construction we’re forced to do, especially in California — a poor imitation of traditional materials
His positive spin: when you’re not doing traditional construction, you can at the very least point to it, point in that direction
So instead of a fakery of traditional art, perhaps it’s pointing to what the real deal is
To me it’s a brand-new medium, an absolute blast to explore
I just love to create

urban.title: THE LARGER PICTURE
urban.note:
Not so concerned with the philosophical implications of ownership of art
Letting go of ownership lets you explore more design options without attaching to them
More importantly: using AI to develop large-scale building programs for small civic cities, at scale and quickly
Good urban frameworks are extremely time-consuming by hand
A tool for rapid iteration of good urban spaces — farther down the line, faster
Overall a win for architecture and urbanism
That’s the end goal, more than the imagery
Only just started controlling these plans to make them coherent and logical — more work will get me there

urban1.note: Workflow developed for urban block layouts

urban2.note: First-iteration floor plans at ultra-high resolution

craft.title: RECENT WORK
craft.note:
A misconception: click a button, instantly get a finished image
The cycle: AI → hand drawing → digital painting → 3D modeling → back to AI

craft2.note:
In reality — an immense amount of digital hand-painting and tablet work
Eliminating AI artifacts and structural flaws
The true value: an architectural eye during final production
Looking compositionally — pulling out leading edges, refining details
Applying principles learned from traditional media and digital painting

workflows1.title: Workflows

proc.c1b: Rendered 98% accurate to the original drawings.
proc.c2b: Rapidly refined toward a polished end.

workflows1.note:
Complete design to render — 98% accurate to the drawings
Sketchy design or schematic to render
Design iterations to render

workflows2.title: Media Workflows

proc.c1b: Loose to tight, dialed in precisely.
proc.c3b: Rough site plan or CAD to watercolor plan.

workflows2.note:
Workflows tailored to watercolor washes and looser plein air aesthetics — dialed in precisely
Newer pencil-sketch workflow — shifts from loose to tight delineation
Rough site plans to landscaped rendering
Bad CAD plan to finally rendered watercolor plan

support.title: 3D Modeling Support
support.note: 3D modeling support from early massing models through render

meetings.title: COLLABORATION PROCESS
meetings.note:
Three short meetings per project works well
One explores, two locks in, three refines

services.title: Consultation & Services
services.note:
Happy to offer consultation and design support at any stage of the pipeline
Early schematic design iterations, massing studies, exploratory concepts
High-end, evocative renderings of designs you have completely resolved
Structured design support, or a rapid Hail Mary for an RFP or competition
Available to collaborate

close.title: TASA.WORKS
close.note:
Invite questions and commissions
End on time

cmrbmhtuv.note:
A program in progress: assign characters, typologies to footprints
They seed into a generator that iterates footprints on an architectural language model
Rapidly iterate real programs for cities — get to footprints faster
